channel.title: ai on Cozi
channel.description: Recent content in ai on Cozi

item.title: Plan Mode: Make Claude Think Before It Types
item.description: There’s a failure mode with AI coding tools that everyone recognizes: you describe a change, the model immediately starts editing files, and three minutes later you’re reverting half of it because it ran off in the wrong direction.

item.title: Subagents: Stop Burning Your Context Window
item.description: Here’s a pattern I kept hitting with Claude Code: I ask it to fix something, it goes off and reads fifteen files to figure out where the problem lives, and by the time it starts actually fixing things, half the context window is gone.

item.title: Claude Code Hooks That Earn Their Keep
item.description: Most of what I tell Claude Code, I only have to say once and it sticks for the session.

item.title: Writing a CLAUDE.md That Actually Helps
item.description: Most CLAUDE.md files I see are either empty or a dumping ground.

item.title: Leveling Up Claude Code with Superpowers
item.description: A while back I wrote about spec-driven development with Spec Kit, which fixed the “the agent built the wrong thing” problem by making me write the spec before any code.

item.title: Spec-Driven Development with GitHub Spec Kit
item.description: I have been using AI coding agents long enough to know the failure mode by heart.
